About.heading: Hi, I'm Rashedul Islam.
About.p1: I engineer high-performance web applications that solve complex business problems. With a deep focus on clean architecture and modern user experiences, I build products that are fast, accessible, and ready to scale.

About.p3: I wake up every day excited to learn new things, build great products, and grow as a developer. Delivering code that buyers and teams love is my ultimate goal.

Testimonials.description: Here’s what founders, marketers, and creative directors have to say about working together.

item1.title: We went from Figma to production in 11 days
item1.message: We'd been sitting on designs for two months because our last dev kept pushing timelines. Aayush had a staging link in 4 days and we were live in 11. The site loads in under a second and our bounce rate dropped 35% the first week. Wish we'd found him sooner.
item1.name: Marcus T.

item2.title: Finally a developer who actually listens
item2.message: I'm not technical at all, and past devs made me feel stupid for asking questions. Aayush sent Loom walkthroughs after every milestone so I always knew exactly where things stood. When I changed my mind about the checkout flow halfway through, he didn't push back — just adjusted and shipped it better than what I originally asked for.
item2.name: Lauren K.

item3.title: Our Core Web Vitals went from red to green overnight
item3.message: We hired Aayush to rebuild our marketing site on Next.js. The old WordPress site was scoring 38 on PageSpeed. His build scores 97. He set up the CMS integration so our content team can publish without touching code. Solid architecture, clean codebase — the kind of work I'd expect from a senior engineer.
item3.name: Daniel R.

item4.title: He caught problems we didn't even know we had
item4.message: We hired him to redesign our product pages. During the build he noticed our image pipeline was serving uncompressed files and our mobile nav was broken on Safari. Fixed both without being asked. That's the kind of developer you want — someone who cares about the whole product, not just their ticket.
item4.name: James L.

item5.title: He turned our messy brief into something beautiful
item5.message: We gave Aayush a mood board and some rough wireframes — honestly, they were half-baked. He came back with a prototype that was cleaner and more thoughtful than what we'd imagined. The animations feel intentional, the typography is perfect, and three clients have asked us who built it.
item5.name: Sofia M.

item6.title: We've shipped 4 projects together now
item6.message: First project was a simple landing page. Then he rebuilt our client portal, added a blog with headless CMS, and just finished an analytics dashboard. Every project is ahead of schedule. He's basically our dev team at this point. If you're a small agency that needs a reliable build partner, stop looking.
item6.name: Ryan H.

Footer.desc1: A full-stack developer, freelancer & problem solver.
Footer.desc2: Thanks for checking out my site!

exp1.desc1: Led the migration of a legacy monolithic architecture to a modern Next.js stack, improving page load speeds by 40%.
exp1.desc2: Architected and implemented a scalable design system using Tailwind CSS and Radix UI.
exp1.desc3: Mentored junior developers and established code review best practices.

exp2.desc1: Collaborated closely with designers to build pixel-perfect, highly interactive web applications.
exp2.desc2: Implemented complex animations using Framer Motion and GSAP.
exp2.desc3: Reduced CSS payload by 60% by refactoring styles into functional utility classes.

exp3.desc1: Developed the initial MVP for a B2B SaaS platform, securing $2M in seed funding.
exp3.desc2: Integrated RESTful APIs and managed complex state using Redux.
exp3.desc3: Ensured cross-browser compatibility and responsive design across all devices.

UsesPage.hardwareDesc: My daily driver setup is focused on maximizing productivity while maintaining a clean, minimal aesthetic. A powerful machine, combined with the right peripherals, makes long coding sessions a breeze.
UsesPage.keychronDesc: Tactile switches for the ultimate typing experience.
UsesPage.devToolsDesc: My terminal is my second home. I heavily rely on a keyboard-centric workflow to navigate codebase, write code, and manage server infrastructure without ever touching the mouse.
UsesPage.neovimDesc: Blazing fast, highly customized terminal workflow.
UsesPage.arcDesc: A fundamentally better way to use the web and manage spaces.
UsesPage.techStackDesc: I love building with modern tools that provide great developer experience and deliver highly optimized, accessible, and stunning user interfaces.
UsesPage.reactDesc: The undisputed kings of modern web development.

AttributionPage.creditDesc: This portfolio stands on the shoulders of giants. Here are the amazing creators, open-source projects, and tools that made building this possible.
AttributionPage.typographyDesc: Typography forms the backbone of this portfolio's premium feel. I chose typefaces that blend editorial elegance with modern digital aesthetics.
AttributionPage.frameworksDesc: The incredible open-source frameworks and libraries that power the core logic, styling, and fluid animations of the entire application.
AttributionPage.nextjsDesc: The core React framework powering the entire application architecture and routing.
AttributionPage.tailwindDesc: The utility-first CSS framework used for all rapid and responsive styling.
AttributionPage.framerDesc: The incredible animation library used for the fluid interactions, scroll reveals, and liquid glass page transitions.
AttributionPage.inspirationDesc: Great design is rarely created in a vacuum. These are the amazing creators and platforms that heavily inspired the visual direction of this portfolio.
AttributionPage.aceternityDesc: Massive inspiration for many of the premium, interactive components like this very Timeline and the World Map.
AttributionPage.magicuiDesc: Inspiration for the sleek, modern animations, borders, and glassmorphism micro-interactions.
